raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally involves repairing the contribution framing around end-to-end modeling, pipeline comparison, and supervision claims.","R1.2":"The artifact contains load-bearing internal contradictions between the claimed unified end-to-end model and the described sequential multi-component pipeline, as well as between supervised training and supervision-free claims.","R1.3":"The requested improvement requires checking that reported results and attributed gains support the stated explanation and generalization claims.","R1.5":"Draft improvement reasonably includes reorganizing the paper-style narrative so motivation, method, training, results, and claims form a coherent arc.","R1.6":"A broad request to improve a draft centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about which kinds of improvement are intended by the broad delegation.","RX.2":"The improvement responsibility must avoid amplifying unsupported claims such as firstness, supervision-free training, or causal explanations for gains.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities rather than executing edits or evaluating outside literature beyond the delegation.","RX.4":"The projection should account for avoiding downstream scholarly harm from misleading methodological or empirical claims.","RX.5":"The projection must preserve traceability to the artifact’s stated claims, methods, training setup, and reported metrics."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally involves repairing the contribution framing around end-to-end modeling, pipeline comparison, and supervision claims.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains load-bearing internal contradictions between the claimed unified end-to-end model and the described sequential multi-component pipeline, as well as between supervised training and supervision-free claims.
rationale.R1.3: The requested improvement requires checking that reported results and attributed gains support the stated explanation and generalization claims.
rationale.R1.5: Draft improvement reasonably includes reorganizing the paper-style narrative so motivation, method, training, results, and claims form a coherent arc.
rationale.R1.6: A broad request to improve a draft centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about which kinds of improvement are intended by the broad delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The improvement responsibility must avoid amplifying unsupported claims such as firstness, supervision-free training, or causal explanations for gains.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities rather than executing edits or evaluating outside literature beyond the delegation.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for avoiding downstream scholarly harm from misleading methodological or empirical claims.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must preserve traceability to the artifact’s stated claims, methods, training setup, and reported metrics.
